target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Public Contract Rationale

- `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` is optional and additive. Existing providers with quota windows keep using `windows`; shared formatters only fall back to `summary` when a provider has no windows and no error.
- `models.authStatus.usage.summary` is optional and mirrors the same snapshot field so API-key balance providers can expose account-state text without changing existing window payloads.
- The provider-usage SDK export keeps DeepSeek-specific HTTP parsing inside the bundled DeepSeek provider path instead of requiring plugins to import internal `src` modules.
- Tests cover summary-only formatting, DeepSeek auth-status enrichment, provider loading, and existing window-based formatting behavior.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence: Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn-applies-thinking-default.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Current Branch Note

This branch was refreshed onto current `upstream/main` (`95890fe150`) in merge commit `e2e13557ce0d43c6943ab274dbc3a85f5ea47aed`; follow-up commits `2aa4a69943` and `797c52ea95` only align subagent config typing and clarify usage docs.

A local `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`/commit hook attempt was blocked by registry fetch timeouts while installing newly introduced upstream binary packages. The SDK baseline was regenerated and checked directly with `node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts`; GitHub CI will re-run the full matrix on the pushed merge head.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because two independent delivery paths were active simultaneously:

1. **Draft stream path**: `onPartialReply` → `bot.api.sendMessage()` sent preview text immediately
2. **ACP projector path**: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` blocked ACP block delivery but couldn't reach the upstream draft stream
3. **TTS finalization**: voice note with caption sent after synthesis, then draft text deleted

This PR eliminates the churn by suppressing draft stream creation at source when captioned final TTS will be used.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. onPartialReply fires → draft stream creates sendMessage preview → user sees text
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered → draft text deleted
  Result: user sees text flash then disappear = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan() → willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true
  2. canStreamAnswerDraft = false → NO draft stream created
  3. TTS synthesis runs silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.ts` | **New** shared helper `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan()` combining channel capability, TTS mode, and TTS status checks |
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts` | **New** 4 unit tests for the shared helper |
| `src/plugin-sdk/tts-runtime.ts` | Re-export `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan` for extension access |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | Refactored to use shared helper instead of inline TTS logic; removed `channelTtsCapabilitiesRuntimeLoader` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 regression tests for deferred settlement, block suppression, abort fallback |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Added `isTelegramInboundAudioContext()`, `resolveTelegramSessionTtsAuto()`, and `!willUseCaptionedFinalTts` guard on `canStreamAnswerDraft` |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` | 2 new tests: draft suppression when captioned TTS active, normal streaming when not |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | Block delivery suppression support |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator for suppressed text fallback |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 58 passed (58)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts
 ✓ 4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
 ✓ 96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
 OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

Total: 170 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message flash briefly (2-5 seconds), then it disappeared and a voice note with caption appeared. Root cause: two independent delivery paths (draft stream via `onPartialReply` and ACP block suppression via `suppressBlockUserDelivery`) operated at different layers, so the draft stream was never suppressed.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `e33c474a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message ("老板好，好久不见！有什么需要效劳的？") AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption. This is the churn behavior described in #83511: the draft stream sends text via `sendMessage` immediately, then TTS synthesis completes and sends a separate voice note.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch (commit `e33c474a`) via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  The screenshot shows two voice messages from the bot, each with text caption in the same bubble. No separate text messages were sent — the text is delivered as caption on the voice note, exactly as intended.

- **Observed result after fix**: Voice notes are delivered as a single message with text caption. No draft text message is created or deleted. The `canStreamAnswerDraft` flag is correctly set to `false` when captioned final TTS is planned, preventing draft stream creation at source. Non-TTS conversations continue to use normal streaming. Other channels (Discord, Slack, etc.) are unaffected.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS behavior (tested in DM only). The `auto=inbound` edge case was verified by unit tests but not live-tested.

Closes #83511


---

## Additional fix: dispatch-from-config text deferral + draft stream suppression (e33c474a)

The original fix addressed only `dispatch-acp.ts` and `bot-message-dispatch.ts`. Live testing revealed two additional issues:

### Issue 1: Missing text deferral in `dispatch-from-config.ts`
The user's agent used the `dispatch-from-config` path (not ACP), which lacked the `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate. Block text was delivered before TTS voice synthesis completed, causing split messages.

**Fix**: Added `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate in `dispatch-from-config.ts` with:
- Block text accumulation and deferral
- Final payload text merging when resolver returns text
- Deferred text recovery on abort/error paths via `routeReplyToOriginating`
- 8 targeted tests (189 total in dispatch-from-config.test.ts)

### Issue 2: Draft stream (`onPartialReply`) operating independently
The Telegram draft stream sent real-time text previews via `editMessageText` independently from block delivery suppression, causing text to flicker before voice+caption arrived.

**Fix**: Suppress `onPartialReply` when `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` is active:
```typescript
onPartialReply: shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts
  ? undefined
  : wrapProgressCallback(params.replyOptions?.onPartialReply),
```

### Additional files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | Text deferral gate + draft stream suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 9 new tests for deferral, merge, abort, error, routing, and draft suppression |
| `src/tts/tts-config.ts` | Type definitions for captioned TTS |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts` | TTS voice caption merge in lane deliverer |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts` | 2 new tests for lane deliverer merge path |

### Verified fix (e33c474a)

![After fix — single voice message with caption, no text flicker](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/A1CC95B74A5340F384CAB69EDB482F34.jpg)

### Test results (all passing)

```
dispatch-from-config.test.ts:     189 passed
dispatch-from-config.acp-abort:    16 passed
dispatch-acp.test.ts:              59 passed
lane-delivery.test.ts:             34 passed
Total:                            298 passed
```

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(configure): migrate stale Codex defaults after OpenAI auth
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a configure/auth mismatch where OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex auth could preserve a stale legacy `codex/gpt-5.5` default while adding the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model.
- Keeps the selected primary model aligned with the canonical OpenAI provider id returned by auth.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #88456 reports successful ChatGPT/Codex device pairing followed by config returning to the menu with an unusable default model outside the selected provider model set.

What is the intended outcome?

- After OpenAI/Codex auth returns `openai/gpt-5.5`, a matching stale `codex/gpt-5.5` or `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5` instead of being preserved.
- Existing default preservation is unchanged for unrelated providers.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not redesign the OpenAI/Codex model picker or change live OAuth/device-code transport.
- This does not remove legacy model-map entries; it only prevents a matching stale legacy ref from remaining the primary default.

What does success look like?

- The configured primary model is present under the canonical OpenAI model entry after auth.
- Existing non-OpenAI defaults are still preserved when provider auth only adds another available model.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The preserve-existing-default guard in `src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts`.
- The regression test proving `codex/gpt-5.5` migrates to `openai/gpt-5.5` only for the matching OpenAI auth path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #88456

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #88456

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No direct maintainer request beyond the linked issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI/Codex auth no longer leaves a matching stale legacy Codex ref as the default primary while canonical OpenAI refs are added to the model map.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, invoking the actual provider-auth config application path with a redacted local token/provider setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the auth-choice config application path directly with before primary `codex/gpt-5.5`, selected auth model `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.cache/corepack PNPM_HOME=/home/node/.local/share/pnpm node --import tsx --input-type=module
  {
    "beforePrimary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
    "afterPrimary": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "allowlistKeys": [
      "codex/gpt-5.5",
      "openai/gpt-5.5"
    ],
    "note": {
      "title": "Model configured",
      "message": "Default model set to openai/gpt-5.5"
    }
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The primary default changes to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` while both legacy and canonical model entries remain present in `agents.defaults.models`.
- What was not tested: A full live ChatGPT/Codex device-code browser pairing against a real account from this container.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The worker cannot complete the interactive account authorization step without a user/browser handoff. The proof exercises the same config mutation path after auth returns a canonical OpenAI default.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The linked issue shows the broken state: primary `codex/gpt-5.5` with canonical OpenAI entries in the model allowlist.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.prompt-auth-config.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added provider-auth coverage for `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` with existing primary `codex/gpt-5.5` and OpenAI auth default `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- The test verifies the primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5`, the canonical model entry is present, and the provider selection hook still runs.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The stale legacy default could be preserved as primary even though OpenAI auth added canonical `openai/*` entries, leaving the session default outside the selected provider allowlist.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; regression coverage was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- The preserve-existing-default behavior during provider auth.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The new exception is narrow: it only applies when OpenAI auth returns an `openai/*` model and the previous primary is a matching legacy `codex/*` or `openai-codex/*` ref for the same model id.
- Existing coverage still verifies unrelated existing defaults remain preserved.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI.
- Optional maintainer/live-environment confirmation of the full ChatGPT/Codex device-code prompt flow.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Initial Real behavior proof gate failure was addressed by replacing prose-only evidence with copied terminal output.

target.title: fix(configure): migrate stale Codex defaults after OpenAI auth
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a configure/auth mismatch where OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex auth could preserve a stale legacy `codex/gpt-5.5` default while adding the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model.
- Keeps the selected primary model aligned with the canonical OpenAI provider id returned by auth.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #88456 reports successful ChatGPT/Codex device pairing followed by config returning to the menu with an unusable default model outside the selected provider model set.

What is the intended outcome?

- After OpenAI/Codex auth returns `openai/gpt-5.5`, a matching stale `codex/gpt-5.5` or `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5` instead of being preserved.
- Existing default preservation is unchanged for unrelated providers.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not redesign the OpenAI/Codex model picker or change live OAuth/device-code transport.
- This does not remove legacy model-map entries; it only prevents a matching stale legacy ref from remaining the primary default.

What does success look like?

- The configured primary model is present under the canonical OpenAI model entry after auth.
- Existing non-OpenAI defaults are still preserved when provider auth only adds another available model.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The preserve-existing-default guard in `src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts`.
- The regression test proving `codex/gpt-5.5` migrates to `openai/gpt-5.5` only for the matching OpenAI auth path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #88456

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #88456

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No direct maintainer request beyond the linked issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI/Codex auth no longer leaves a matching stale legacy Codex ref as the default primary while canonical OpenAI refs are added to the model map.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, invoking the actual provider-auth config application path with a redacted local token/provider setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the auth-choice config application path directly with before primary `codex/gpt-5.5`, selected auth model `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.cache/corepack PNPM_HOME=/home/node/.local/share/pnpm node --import tsx --input-type=module
  {
    "beforePrimary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
    "afterPrimary": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "allowlistKeys": [
      "codex/gpt-5.5",
      "openai/gpt-5.5"
    ],
    "note": {
      "title": "Model configured",
      "message": "Default model set to openai/gpt-5.5"
    }
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The primary default changes to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` while both legacy and canonical model entries remain present in `agents.defaults.models`.
- What was not tested: A full live ChatGPT/Codex device-code browser pairing against a real account from this container.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The worker cannot complete the interactive account authorization step without a user/browser handoff. The proof exercises the same config mutation path after auth returns a canonical OpenAI default.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The linked issue shows the broken state: primary `codex/gpt-5.5` with canonical OpenAI entries in the model allowlist.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.prompt-auth-config.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added provider-auth coverage for `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` with existing primary `codex/gpt-5.5` and OpenAI auth default `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- The test verifies the primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5`, the canonical model entry is present, and the provider selection hook still runs.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The stale legacy default could be preserved as primary even though OpenAI auth added canonical `openai/*` entries, leaving the session default outside the selected provider allowlist.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; regression coverage was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- The preserve-existing-default behavior during provider auth.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The new exception is narrow: it only applies when OpenAI auth returns an `openai/*` model and the previous primary is a matching legacy `codex/*` or `openai-codex/*` ref for the same model id.
- Existing coverage still verifies unrelated existing defaults remain preserved.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI.
- Optional maintainer/live-environment confirmation of the full ChatGPT/Codex device-code prompt flow.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Initial Real behavior proof gate failure was addressed by replacing prose-only evidence with copied terminal output.

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; registry assembly must reject disabled workspace channel registrations; trusted setup-entry registration errors must be recorded without aborting healthy sibling plugins. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs live at loader/registry/catalog boundaries and require real plugin import behavior plus registration-mode assertions. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `only setup-loads a disabled channel plugin when the caller scopes to the selected plugin` still covers the non-workspace path. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Untrusted workspace channel shadows no longer execute during setup-only scoped loads. - Trusted workspace channel plugins remain available to setup-time scoped loading. - A broken trusted setup-entry channel registration now surfaces as a plugin diagnostic instead of stopping sibling plugin loading. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Node 22 local repo checkout - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader / channel plugin registry / channel setup catalog - Relevant config (redacted): workspace plugins enabled; untrusted plugin omitted from allowlist ### Steps 1. Create a workspace channel plugin fixture with `channels` metadata and a side-effect marker write in `register()`. 2. Load it through `loadOpenClawPlugins({ includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, forceSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, onlyPluginIds: [...] })`. 3. Compare disabled workspace behavior against trusted workspace behavior. 4. Load a setup-entry channel whose channel config getter throws during `registerChannel` alongside a healthy sibling plugin. ### Expected - Disabled workspace channel plugin stays disabled and does not execute. - Trusted workspace channel plugin remains available to setup-only loading. - A throwing trusted setup-entry channel records a diagnostic and does not prevent sibling plugins from loading. ### Actual - This branch matches the expected behavior. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets in test output - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: untrusted/trusted workspace scoped setup-only regressions, disabled workspace registry guard, trusted-catalog filtering semantics, onboarding channel setup e2e catalog mock compatibility, `channels add` workspace shadow fallback, #85774 setup-entry `registerChannel` error isolation, `pnpm build`, and final `pnpm check:changed`. - Edge cases checked: trusted workspace override still loads in setup-only mode; non-workspace disabled plugin behavior stays unchanged; setup wizard discovery still keeps workspace-only install candidates visible without treating them as installed/executable. - What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end channel setup outside the loader test harness. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) No. This is an intentional security hardening break for disabled workspace channel plugins that were previously reachable through setup-only scoped loads. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No automatic migration. Operators who intentionally develop or run a workspace/linked channel plugin must explicitly enable/trust that plugin before setup-time code can run. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: use the normal plugin enable/trust flow for the workspace plugin before running channel setup. The behavior is documented in `docs/cli/plugins.md`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a trusted workspace plugin could accidentally get blocked if the trust check were too broad. - Mitigation: regression coverage explicitly keeps the trusted workspace scoped-load path green. - Risk: setup discovery could accidentally hide workspace-only install candidates. - Mitigation: trusted-catalog and discovery tests keep the strict execution list separate from the lenient setup wizard display list. - Risk: an existing disabled workspace channel plugin that relied on setup-only execution now fails closed. - Mitigation: this is the intended security behavior; the docs now state that workspace-origin plugins are not imported or executed until explicitly enabled/trusted. ### Proof 1: Pre-Fix Reproduction on origin/main - Behavior addressed: Untrusted workspace channel plugin (evi1-channel-probe) executes its register() function via the setup-only scoped load path when includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true is passed to loadOpenClawPlugins, bypassing the trust boundary. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, checkout SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch --depth=1 origin main && git checkout FETCH_HEAD` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) Deploy evi1-channel-probe plugin to /tmp/proof1-workspace/.openclaw/extensions/evi1-channel-probe/; (4) Confirm /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence absent; (5) Run `node probe-untrusted.mjs` with onlyPluginIds=["evi1-channel-probe"], includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins=true, activate=false, cache=false; (6) Check /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence existence. - Evidence after fix: Marker file /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence CREATED with content "evi1-probe-executed\n". Plugin status="disabled" with channelSetups=[{id:"evi1-channel-probe"}] — the setup-only path imported and executed the untrusted workspace plugin code. - Observed result after fix: On origin/main (pre-fix), the untrusted workspace channel plugin code ran despite being disabled. The plugin appeared in channelSetups (not channels), status was "disabled", yet register() executed and created the marker file. This confirms the bypass vulnerability. - What was not tested: CLI blackbox path, real channels add flow ### Proof 2: Post-Fix Verification on PR Head - Behavior addressed: The PR fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugins from executing during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected at registry assembly; trusted workspace plugins with plugins.allow still work; broken trusted setup-entry registerChannel records a diagnostic without aborting sibling plugins. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=df29879e2faef7ac8794cba519c1988aff410316a3718d90c6f638cd9977ce15, lease=cbx_0f687f5da081, checkout SHA=b05a7b14fe9bd6c119b3b7fe60a1967d0987804c (PR head at time of test; refreshed #86953 head is `2a9a2d79fd498be5fc62e83634a544f873b67b4c`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch origin pull/82579/head:pr-head && git checkout pr-head` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) `grep -r "channel registration rejected for disabled workspace plugin" dist/` — fix code found in dist/loader-DM-dphfh.js; (4) Deploy evi1-channel-probe (untrusted), confirm marker ABSENT after probe; (5) Deploy evi1-trusted-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm marker EXISTS; (6) Deploy evi1-broken-register + evi1-sibling-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm sibling marker EXISTS and diagnostic recorded; (7) Run focused tests: `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts` (142 pass), `pnpm test src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts` (2 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts` (5 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts` (6 pass); (8) `pnpm check:changed` — 0 errors. - Evidence after fix: Untrusted probe: MARKER_ABSENT, exit code 0 (clean block, not crash). target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
